title: A Trip to the Museum
summary: A narrator describes her visit to a local history museum, exploring different rooms, learning about the past, and enjoying a quiet afternoon away from the busy city.
body: Last Saturday, I decided to visit the history museum in the city centre. I had never been there before, so I was quite excited. When I arrived, I bought a ticket at the front desk and picked up a free map of the building. The museum had three floors, and each floor had a different theme. On the ground floor, there were old tools and furniture from hundreds of years ago. I was surprised by how large some of the farming tools were. On the second floor, I found an exhibition about the lives of ordinary people in the past. There were photographs, letters, and everyday objects that told their stories. I spent a long time reading the information boards because I found them really interesting. On the top floor, there was a special display about local artists, and I liked the colourful paintings very much. After about two hours, I went to the café on the ground floor and had a cup of tea and a slice of cake. I sat by the window and watched people walking past outside. When I left, I felt calm and happy. Museums are wonderful places because they help us understand where we come from.

annotations.exampleEn: She was quite excited about her first trip abroad.

annotations.meaningVi: quầy lễ tân, quầy tiếp tân
annotations.exampleEn: Please collect your key from the front desk.
The supermarket is on the ground floor of the building.
The museum displayed old farming tools used centuries ago.
History is not just about kings — it is also about ordinary people.
The information boards explained the history of the castle.
There was a special display of traditional costumes.
The gallery featured works by local artists.
Would you like a slice of cake with your tea?

annotations.meaningVi: cảm thấy bình yên, thư thái
annotations.exampleEn: After the walk in the park, she felt calm and relaxed.

annotations.meaningVi: hiểu nguồn gốc của chúng ta
annotations.exampleEn: Studying history helps us understand where we come from.

grammarNotes.title: Past simple for completed actions
grammarNotes.bodyVi: Thì quá khứ đơn diễn tả hành động đã hoàn thành trong quá khứ. Trong bài, các động từ như 'decided', 'arrived', 'bought', 'found', 'spent' đều ở dạng quá khứ đơn vì chúng là những việc đã xảy ra và kết thúc vào thứ Bảy tuần trước.
grammarNotes.bodyEn: Use the past simple to describe completed actions in the past. Verbs like 'decided', 'arrived', 'bought', 'found', and 'spent' show that each action was finished at a specific point in the past. Regular verbs add -ed; many common verbs are irregular (buy → bought, find → found).

grammarNotes.title: Had never + past participle (past perfect negative)
grammarNotes.bodyVi: Cấu trúc 'had never + quá khứ phân từ' diễn tả một điều chưa bao giờ xảy ra trước một thời điểm trong quá khứ. Ví dụ: 'I had never been there before' có nghĩa là trước chuyến đi đó, cô ấy chưa từng đến bảo tàng đó.
grammarNotes.bodyEn: The structure 'had never + past participle' (past perfect negative) describes something that had not happened before a point in the past. Example: 'I had never been there before' means that up to that Saturday, she had not visited the museum.

translationVi: Thứ Bảy tuần trước, tôi quyết định đến thăm bảo tàng lịch sử ở trung tâm thành phố. Tôi chưa bao giờ đến đó trước đây, vì vậy tôi khá hào hứng. Khi đến nơi, tôi mua vé ở quầy lễ tân và nhận một tờ bản đồ miễn phí của toà nhà. Bảo tàng có ba tầng và mỗi tầng có một chủ đề khác nhau. Ở tầng trệt, có các công cụ và đồ nội thất cũ từ hàng trăm năm trước. Tôi ngạc nhiên vì một số dụng cụ nông nghiệp lại lớn như vậy. Ở tầng hai, tôi tìm thấy một triển lãm về cuộc sống của những người bình thường trong quá khứ. Có những bức ảnh, thư tín và đồ vật thường ngày kể lại câu chuyện của họ. Tôi đã dành nhiều thời gian đọc các bảng thông tin vì tôi thấy chúng thực sự thú vị. Ở tầng trên cùng, có một triển lãm đặc biệt về các nghệ sĩ địa phương và tôi rất thích những bức tranh đầy màu sắc. Sau khoảng hai tiếng, tôi xuống quán cà phê ở tầng trệt và uống một tách trà và một miếng bánh. Tôi ngồi cạnh cửa sổ và nhìn mọi người đi ngang qua bên ngoài. Khi rời đi, tôi cảm thấy bình yên và vui vẻ. Bảo tàng là những nơi tuyệt vời vì chúng giúp chúng ta hiểu nguồn gốc của mình.

questions.prompt: When did the narrator visit the museum?
questions.explanation: The narrator says 'Last Saturday, I decided to visit the history museum.'
questions.hint: Listen for the day of the week.

questions.prompt: Where is the history museum located?
questions.explanation: The narrator says it is 'the history museum in the city centre.'
questions.hint: The location is mentioned in the first sentence.

questions.prompt: What did the narrator receive for free at the front desk?
questions.explanation: She 'picked up a free map of the building' at the front desk.
questions.hint: It helped her navigate the museum.

questions.prompt: How many floors did the museum have?
questions.explanation: The narrator says 'The museum had three floors.'
questions.hint: She visited the ground floor, second floor, and top floor.

questions.prompt: What was on the ground floor?
questions.explanation: On the ground floor, 'there were old tools and furniture from hundreds of years ago.'
questions.hint: It related to life many centuries ago.

questions.prompt: What surprised the narrator on the ground floor?
options: How large some farming tools were
questions.explanation: She was 'surprised by how large some of the farming tools were.'
questions.hint: She was surprised by the size of something.

questions.prompt: What was the second floor exhibition about?
options: The lives of ordinary people in the past
questions.explanation: She 'found an exhibition about the lives of ordinary people in the past.'
questions.hint: It focused on everyday life, not famous figures.

questions.prompt: Why did the narrator spend a long time reading the information boards?
options: Because the text was very difficult
Because she found them really interesting
Because she was waiting for a friend
Because there was nowhere to sit
questions.explanation: She 'found them really interesting', which is why she spent a long time reading.
questions.hint: She had a positive reaction to the boards.

questions.prompt: What was on the top floor?
options: A special display about local artists
questions.explanation: On the top floor, 'there was a special display about local artists.'
questions.hint: It was related to art and creativity.

questions.prompt: What did the narrator have in the café?
options: Tea and a slice of cake
questions.explanation: She 'had a cup of tea and a slice of cake' in the café.
questions.hint: A hot drink and a sweet snack.

questions.prompt: How did the narrator feel when she left the museum?
questions.explanation: She says 'I felt calm and happy' when she left.
questions.hint: Two positive adjectives describe her mood.

questions.prompt: According to the narrator, why are museums wonderful places?
options: Because they are free to visit
Because they help us understand where we come from
questions.explanation: She concludes: 'Museums are wonderful places because they help us understand where we come from.'

blanks.explanation: 'museum' — the whole passage describes a visit to the history museum.
'excited' — she was quite excited because she had never been there before.
'ticket' — she bought a ticket at the front desk to enter the museum.
'theme' — each floor had a different theme, meaning a different subject or focus.
'furniture' — the ground floor had old tools and furniture from hundreds of years ago.
'exhibition' — an exhibition is a public display of objects or artwork.
'information' — information boards provide facts and explanations about the exhibits.
'artists' — the top floor had a special display about local artists and their colourful paintings.
'café' — after exploring the floors, she went to the café for tea and cake.
'cake' — she had a cup of tea and a slice of cake.
'calm' — she felt calm and happy when she left the museum.

criticalThinkingQuestion: The narrator says museums help us understand where we come from. Do you think it is important to learn about local history? How can knowing your history affect the way you live today?
